title: Cannot build from dockerfile
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

/kind feature

**Description**

Replaced docker for mac with podman. Starting a vscode remote container project does not start anymore. I get the error:

```
STEP 4/4: RUN if [ "$USER_GID" != "1000" ] || [ "$USER_UID" != "1000" ]; then groupmod --gid $USER_GID node && usermod --uid $USER_UID --gid $USER_GID node; fi
exec container process `/bin/sh`: Exec format error
1 error occurred:
	* Status: error building at STEP "RUN if [ "$USER_GID" != "1000" ] || [ "$USER_UID" != "1000" ]; then groupmod --gid $USER_GID node && usermod --uid $USER_UID --gid $USER_GID node; fi": error while running runtime: exit status 1
, Code: 1
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. install podman. `Version:      4.0.3`

2. install docker compose v2.2.3

3. set up a default remote container project on vscode with defaults for nodejs and postgres container.

**Describe the results you received:**

The docker compose build fails on 

```
RUN if [ "$USER_GID" != "1000" ] || [ "$USER_UID" != "1000" ]; then groupmod --gid $USER_GID node && usermod --uid $USER_UID --gid $USER_GID node; fi
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

This works with docker for mac

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.0.3
API Version:  4.0.3
Go Version:   go1.18
Built:        Fri Apr  1 20:58:59 2022

Go Version:   go1.16.14
Built:        Thu Mar  3 20:28:50 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
M1 Mac

comments.body:
Docker-compose.yaml is v3
docker-compose is v2.2.3

```
exec container process `/bin/sh`: Exec format error
````
The error means that your image does not match your host architecture so it cannot execute your commands.
see https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/12899
If we had qemu-user-static installed in the VM it would have worked, although slowly.
@Luap99 this dockerfile is working fine with docker, only throws an error with podman
Most likely this is because they have the package installed, and can run in emulation mode.
